utterance: Oh my gosh, YES! Actually, just last week I completely overhauled everything because I was just SO frustrated with how chaotic dinner time had become. Like, I was coming home exhausted, the kids were hungry and cranky, and I'd just be standing there staring into the fridge with absolutely NO idea what to make. It was terrible! So I finally sat down—I was honestly kind of at my breaking point—and I decided to actually PLAN things out. I know, I know, it sounds simple, but I never used to do it. And you know what? It's been absolutely amazing! I picked out like five meals for the week, made a grocery list, and when I got home on Monday I actually KNEW what I was making. The relief I felt was incredible—like, genuinely, I felt so much less stressed just having that plan in place. My oldest even said dinner was better because I wasn't so frazzled! *laughs* That just made my whole week, honestly.
label: yes

The planning part is supposed to make cooking easier, but if the planning itself is annoying, I'm like "why am I even doing this?"
